scientific_integrity.detail: Scientific integrity held because the archived workflow stayed at the level of study planning, hypothesis framing, and experiment design rather than claiming completed results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package remained on the planning side of the boundary and did not cross into clinical or diagnostic advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The legacy review kept the package aligned with its named analysis library, data structure, or processing workflow.

code_usability.detail: The package is evaluated primarily as a structured deliverable rather than an executable scientific code workflow.

reliability.note: Reliability was softened by the legacy issue 'Improve stress-case output rigor'. Stress and boundary scenarios show weaker consistency

performance_context.note: The archived evaluation left minor headroom in how efficiently the workflow handles heavier planning contexts.

agent_usability.note: The archived review left some headroom in how directly the workflow guides an agent through the planning sequence.

human_usability.note: Human usability reached full score in the archived evaluation.

security.note: Security scored well, though the archived review still left some room to make boundary language even more explicit.

maintainability.note: The package remains maintainable, though the archived review saw modest room to simplify or stabilize its planning logic.
